values.description: The possible values for this variant. Must contain at least two entries — a single-value dimension should be modeled as a flag variant instead. Ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

variants.description: Documents all dimensions of visual or behavioral variation on a component. Each item is either a flag variant (a boolean toggle like 'disabled' or 'full-width') or an enum variant (a set of mutually exclusive options like size: sm | md | lg). Multiple items document orthogonal dimensions that combine independently.

kind.description: Identifies this guideline as a variants spec.

items.description: The variant dimensions. Each item is either a flag (boolean toggle) or an enum (set of values). Ordering is significant — tools SHOULD display dimensions in this order.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this document block. Provides structured info for AI/LLM use: constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords.

component.description: A single reusable UI component. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` array holds modular entries with a `kind` tag — status, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, since, and links. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: anatomy, api, variant, state, design-specifications, guideline, accessibility, and so on. This unified model replaces separate top-level properties for each concern.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a component.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier (e.g., 'button', 'dialog', 'form-field', 'data-table'). MUST be unique within the documentation group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Button', 'Dialog', 'Form Field', 'Data Table').

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this component, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the component-specific kinds (anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications) plus the general kinds (guideline, purpose, accessibility, examples). The `type` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this component: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.

pattern.description: A repeated, multi-component solution to a common UX problem. Patterns sit above individual components and show how those components compose to meet a user need. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` array holds modular entries with a `kind` tag — status, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. The `documentBlock` array holds structured docs as typed entries: `interaction` for flow steps, `guideline` for rules, `accessibility` for a11y specs, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a pattern.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the pattern (e.g., 'error-messaging', 'navigation', 'empty-state', 'form-validation'). MUST be unique within the documentation group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Error Messaging', 'Navigation', 'Empty State').

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this pattern, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Patterns accept the pattern-specific kinds (interactions) plus the general kinds (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, examples). The `type` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Interaction entries rely on order to show the flow in time. Reference the components a pattern uses through a `links` metadata entry (type 'component', name, and role) rather than a dedicated block kind.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this pattern: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.

style.description: The principles, guidelines, scales, and token references that govern a visual attribute across the system — color, typography, spacing, elevation, and the like. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` array holds modular entries with a `kind` tag (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links, and more). The `documentBlock` array holds structured docs as typed entries: principle, scale, guideline, accessibility, and so on.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a style.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the style (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation', 'motion', 'shape'). MUST be unique within the documentation group.

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this style, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the style-specific kinds (principles, scale) plus the general kinds (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). The `type` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Express internal artifact links (such as token group references) through a `links` metadata entry, not as document block entries.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this style: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.

tokenOverride.description: A single token override inside a theme: which token changes, and why. The override value itself lives in the DTCG file; this entry records the intent and reason.

token.description: The identifier of the token being overridden. MUST match the `identifier` of a token defined elsewhere in the system. Token identifiers are unconstrained by a pattern to support DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary').

description.description: Says what changes for this token in this theme and why — e.g., 'Lightened to meet WCAG AAA contrast on the dark background.' The resolved value itself lives in the DTCG file.

theme.description: A named context that adapts the design system's token values. DSDS captures the purpose, usage document blocks, accessibility considerations, and the list of affected tokens. The override values live in the DTCG file that `source` points to. Use themes for color modes (light, dark, high-contrast), density settings (compact, comfortable), brand variants, or product-specific customizations. The token catalog stays the source of truth for token identity, type, and docs. Typed entries in the `metadata` array carry modular metadata with a `kind` tag (status, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links).

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a theme.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the theme (e.g., 'dark', 'high-contrast', 'compact', 'brand-sub-brand').

name.description: Display name for the theme (e.g., 'Dark Mode', 'High Contrast', 'Compact Density').

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

source.description: Pointer to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this theme's token override definitions.

path.description: The dot-path to this theme's group within the DTCG file, if applicable.

overrides.description: Token overrides this theme applies. Each entry says which token changes and why. List only tokens whose values differ from the default; unlisted tokens keep their default values. The override values themselves live in the DTCG file that `source` points to.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this theme, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Themes accept the general kinds only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). The `type` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this theme: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.

token.description: Docs for a single design token: identifier, type, use cases, guidelines, and accessibility considerations. The display `name` property is absent — the `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional. Typed entries in the modular `metadata` array carry shared properties (status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, links). When a token sits inside a token group and omits its own status metadata entry, the parent group's status applies.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token.

identifier.description: The token identifier. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions that use dots, slashes, or other separators (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary'). Also serves as the human-readable display label.

tokenType.description: The type of token aligned with the DTCG spec (e.g., 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'cubicBezier', 'shadow').

source.description: Pointer to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this token's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this token within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text.primary').

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Tokens accept the general kinds only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). The `type` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this token: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.

tokenGroup.description: A group of related design tokens. Token groups are flexible organizational units: a full collection, a family (e.g., all color tokens), a sub-family (e.g., a single hue with its grade scale), or any other logical grouping. Groups can nest to form a deep hierarchy. A top-level group might represent 'color' and hold child groups for 'color-text', 'color-background', and 'color-border', each with individual tokens. This recursive structure mirrors how real design systems organize tokens into families and sub-families. The display `name` property is absent — the `identifier` itself serves as the display label. Description and status are optional. Typed entries in the modular `metadata` array carry shared properties (status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links). When a child omits its own status metadata entry, the parent group's status applies.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a token-group.

identifier.description: The token group identifier. Unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text', 'color.text', 'color/text'). Also serves as the human-readable display label.

tokenType.description: When all tokens in this group share the same DTCG token type, declare it here to avoid repetition on each child. Each token in children MAY override this value. Common values: 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'number', 'shadow'.

source.description: Pointer to the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) definition. DSDS does not duplicate token values — the DTCG file is the source of truth for resolved values, aliases, and type information.

file.description: URI or relative path to the DTCG file containing this group's definition.

path.description: The dot-path to this group within the DTCG file (e.g., 'color.text').

children.description: An ordered array of tokens and/or nested token groups inside this group. Each item MUST be either a token or a tokenGroup. Order matters — tools SHOULD preserve it for display, since it often shows a meaningful progression (e.g., lightest to darkest, or smallest to largest). Children inherit tokenType and status from this group when they omit their own values.

metadata.description: Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a `kind` tag. Kinds include status, since, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this token group, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the general kinds only (guideline, use-cases, accessibility, examples). Use this array for docs that apply to the group as a whole. Place docs for individual tokens on the token inside `children`.

agents.description: AI-ready context for this token: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use.

fileRef.description: A JSON Reference to a DSDS entity or documentation group defined in another file. Tooling (e.g. json-schema-ref-parser) resolves the reference before validation and merge. The URI should include a JSON Pointer fragment to target a specific value within the referenced file (e.g. './button.dsds.json#/entity', './groups/tokens.dsds.json#/documentation/0').

$ref.description: A relative or absolute URI pointing to a DSDS file, optionally with a JSON Pointer fragment identifying the target value within that file.

documentationGroup.description: A named collection of design system documentation entities. Entities are organized into separate typed arrays — components, tokenGroups, themes, styles, and patterns — rather than a single mixed array. Only the group name is required; all entity arrays are optional, so a group may contain any combination of entity types.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation'). This is already a display label — it does NOT have an `identifier` counterpart and need not match a slug pattern.

components.description: Component entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a UI component — its anatomy, variants, states, accessibility guidelines, and usage rules. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

tokenGroups.description: Token group entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a collection of related design tokens — colors, spacing, typography scales, etc. Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

themes.description: Theme entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a theme — a named set of token overrides that customize the visual appearance of the system (e.g., light/dark mode, brand variations). Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

styles.description: Style entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a reusable visual style — a named combination of token values that can be applied to elements (e.g., elevation levels, text styles). Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.

patterns.description: Pattern entities in this documentation group. Each item describes a UX pattern — a reusable solution to a common design problem, composed of one or more components (e.g., form validation, empty states, navigation flows). Items may be inlined or referenced via a $ref object pointing to another DSDS file.
